reviews.review_description: Can you add easy mode? Right now it's way too easy to die.

You keep plunging into the darkness with monsters constantly running towards your direction. You want to try to collect as many teddy bears as possible as you dodge and weave through the environment and avoiding enemies. Some are freakier than others but the head and arm ghouls get me a bit. Just disturbing looking out the corner of your eyes and seeing those things fly past you. Haven’t gotten too far in it but I enjoy it and it works pretty good on the Go with very minimal performance problems.

When it comes to games, I’m usually the one championing for increased difficulty levels. I grew up with the original Nintendo games that were designed to ensure in the later levels that completing the game would not come without serious challenges. There was a real sense of accomplishment when beating those games. I feel like nowadays many games are just merely glorified walkthroughs with some hiccups along the way. So you would think I’d appreciate a game this difficult. Well, not like this. While I’m all for a game embracing challenge the more you advance, there’s something comical about insane difficulty in the opening seconds. Seriously, I could not last more than 10-15 seconds on any of my numerous attempts. That may speak partly to my lack of skill or my level of patience, but games should also work towards balance as well as difficulty. A game should be inviting in the opening segments and then hook you as it gets progressively harder. Unfortunately, that is not the case here. It looks great in the preview and it does have a nice price tag. I see a potentially great game here but when you are this frustrated this early on, the tendency is to move on to something else. Maybe you would fare better than I did, but I cannot recommend something I can’t play.
